Blue Ridge Foodshed Fund

33314415-10e1-8179-a45f-f544ad3c28a5, 33314415-10e1-81f3-995b-de649d3139b4, 33314415-10e1-81c0-8cf5-c4d11469fd45, 33314415-10e1-812b-8b92-d1e82347260c, 33314415-10e1-8148-aac9-cbf787585b08, 33314415-10e1-811f-9727-c86a6a1c3cc5
network_affiliation
[[BioFi Project]]
type
organization
location_lat
35.6
background_section
## Background The Blue Ridge Mountains are among the oldest and most biodiverse landscapes on Earth—[over 1.2 billion years old](https://youtu.be/nowoYmBRijw), older than the Atlantic Ocean. Cherokee people and their ancestors have stewarded these mountains for roughly 12,000 years. In September 2024, Hurricane Helene tore through the region and exposed a structural tension: western North Carolina still hosts more than [5,000 family farms](https://static1.squarespace.com/static/683a3b865372721c08d6b523/t/68fbfecbe9ab627ddbe7016c/1761345227246/BRFF+Profile.pdf), numerous aggregators and distributors, and [40 farmers' markets](https://static1.squarespace.com/static/683a3b865372721c08d6b523/t/68fbfecbe9ab627ddbe7016c/1761345227246/BRFF+Profile.pdf), yet only about **4%** of food purchased locally is produced in the region—underscoring how fragile long supply chains are and why many residents are recommitting to bioregional food sovereignty. ### Origins and Network The Blue Ridge Foodshed Trust was formed as a nonprofit corporation with a mission to rapidly localize the regional food supply. The Trust brings funding and resources into the region through trusted relationships with mission-aligned partners. Rooted in the Western North Carolina community, the Trust serves as a valuable intermediary with deep knowledge of the foodshed's challenges, needs, and assets.
Sources
33314415-10e1-812d-89ae-dd798bef85d0, 33314415-10e1-81c5-b678-d3bda0742e07

Southern Blue Ridge, Appalachia, USA
activities

description
Blue Ridge Foodshed Trust emerged in the aftermath of Hurricane Helen as a response to the mounting instability of the global food system and the deep vulnerabilities of rural communities. The Trust's mission is to localize food systems by supporting small-scale farmers, building regenerative distribution infrastructure, and addressing food access through community-centered investment.
id
blue-ridge-foodshed-fund
vision_legacy_section
## Vision and Legacy Localizing western North Carolina’s food system offers measurable returns in four areas: economic vitality, public health, ecological repair, and community resilience. The Trust treats those returns as mutually reinforcing—farm income, eater access, watershed health, and civic capacity rising together when capital, land, and governance align around producers.
website
https://www.foodshedtrust.earth/
bioregional_context
The Blue Ridge bioregion spans the ancient Appalachian Mountains of the eastern United States, encompassing parts of Virginia, North Carolina, and Tennessee. Characterized by temperate forests, rolling hills, and biodiverse watersheds, the region has long supported resilient agricultural traditions and community-based food systems.

Bioregional Organizer
